title: Sony accelerates abandonment of physical media and will remove 500 digital movies from PlayStation
summary: The company reduces costs by eliminating the disc drive in PS6, while archivists struggle to preserve PS3 data and more than 500 Studio Canal titles will be removed from user libraries in 2026.

title: Intel quietly raises prices of Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Core Ultra 5 250K Plus
summary: Official product pages already reflect an increase of up to $50 on Intel's flagship processors for the DIY market.

title: Anthropic cuts Claude Code system prompt by 80% to adapt to Fable 5 models
summary: The company claims the new models 'want a smaller system prompt', sparking an intense debate about AI control in the United States.

title: Anthropic explores custom chip manufacturing with Samsung without abandoning Nvidia
summary: The AI company holds advanced talks with Samsung to design custom silicon, while ensuring Nvidia will remain its main hardware partner.

title: OpenAI offers US government 5% of its shares to create an AI sovereign wealth fund
summary: Sam Altman proposes that all leading AI labs contribute to a public prosperity fund in the style of Alaska, amid regulatory negotiations over the launch of GPT-5.6.

title: Intel resumes production of its 13th and 14th Gen Core CPUs exclusively for the Chinese market
summary: The company revives its processors for the DIY channel in China, aiming to clear its inventory in the Asian mid-range segment.

title: Nvidia offers new financing vehicle that swaps cloud compute for a share of revenue
summary: The chip giant proposes to AI companies an optional model where it finances hardware in exchange for tokens on their future cloud revenue.

title: Vulnerability in Apple's Hide My Email exposes users' real addresses for a year
summary: The flaw, known to Cupertino but unpatched, allows attackers to unmask email addresses protected by the service.

title: TV show tracking app TV Time will permanently shut down on July 15, 2026
summary: The parent company announces it will focus on artificial intelligence, leaving its 22 million users without the leading TV tracking platform.

title: Microsoft launches 'Frontier Company' with $2.5 billion and 6,000 AI engineers for enterprises
summary: The new division will embed its engineers directly into enterprise clients to accelerate adoption of Copilot and generative AI.

title: Philips introduces Evnia 27M4N monitors with triple mode: HD 540Hz, FHD 360Hz, and QHD 275Hz
summary: The new 27-inch panels allow switching between three resolutions and refresh rates, aimed at competitive gamers.

title: Prototype of AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX appears with red PCB and only 16GB memory
summary: An engineering sample found on Reddit shows an unreleased configuration of the flagship GPU, without a backplate and with specs close to the RX 7900 GRE.

title: Microsoft halts Game Pass agreements and prepares a tool to digitize physical Xbox games
summary: Amid internal restructuring, Xbox pauses new third-party agreements while developing a feature to convert discs into digital copies.

title: Micron invests $250 million to avoid lawsuits over high memory prices while cheap PCs disappear
summary: The memory maker seeks to appease the Trump administration with a major investment, as high DRAM prices drive low-cost computers out of the market.

title: EU confirms record €4.1 billion fine against Google for Android monopoly abuse
summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union definitively ratifies the penalty, closing the case against the tech giant for its dominance in the mobile operating system market.

title: Meta plans to rent out its AI compute power, sending sector stocks down
summary: The 'Meta Compute' project would put the company in direct competition with AWS, causing an earthquake in the cloud industry.

title: Tesla reports huge sales increase in second quarter of 2025
summary: Elon Musk's company beats market expectations and regains momentum in the electric vehicle sector.

title: China opens world's first AI-driven personalized cancer vaccine factory
summary: The facility will use artificial intelligence to design cancer vaccines tailored to each patient's genetic profile.

title: BYD will install a network of 1,500 kW superchargers in Spain, copying Tesla's model
summary: The Chinese manufacturer announces a massive deployment of ultra-fast chargers in the country by 2027, following the strategy of its US competitor.

title: US government suffers massive new cyberattack
summary: Federal authorities confirm they have been victims of a large-scale computer intrusion.
